title: Tecoma arequipensis
intro: Tecoma arequipensis, also known as Arequipa Yellow Bells, is a flowering plant species in the family Bignoniaceae, native to the Andes mountain range in Peru. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of digestive issues, respiratory problems, and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of Tecoma arequipensis include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic compounds, such as tecomanine, tecomin, and quercetin, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Tecoma arequipensis is often used to make herbal teas, infusions, and extracts, as well as being incorporated into traditional medicine and pharmaceutical preparations.
taxonomy: Tecoma arequipensis is a species of flowering plant in the family Bignoniaceae. This plant is commonly found in the southwestern regions of South America. Tecoma arequipensis is a member of the genus Tecoma, which comprises a group of tropical and subtropical flowering plants. The plant's classification includes being a part of the order Lamiales and the clade asterids. It is closely related to other species in the same family, such as the trumpet vine and the yellow bells.
